data.title: Balancing Near-Term Headwinds and Long-Term Potential: Maintaining a Hold on Forrester
data.description:
William Blair analyst Andrew Nicholas has maintained their neutral stance on FORR stock, giving a Hold rating on February 13.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Andrew Nicholas has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors that balance near‑term challenges with longer‑term potential. Forrester’s fourth-quarter results and newly issued 2026 guidance both came in weaker than his expectations, reflecting ongoing pressure on contract value, softer end markets, and margin compression that collectively weigh on near‑term earnings power.

At the same time, he acknowledges early signs of progress that could support improved performance over time, including better client retention, a greater mix of multiyear contracts, and encouraging initial demand for the AI Access offering. The recently announced restructuring, including workforce reductions and the planned exit from the underperforming strategy consulting business, is intended to realign costs and refocus on core CV growth. Taken together, these elements justify a neutral stance while the company works to stabilize trends and execute on its strategic initiatives.

According to TipRanks, Nicholas is an analyst with an average return of -14.3% and a 26.83% success rate. Nicholas covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as Equifax, Exponent, and CBIZ.

data.title: Air Canada: Improving Fundamentals, Margin Expansion Potential, and Aeroplan Strength Support Buy Rating and Higher Target
data.description:
Thomas Fitzgerald CFA, an analyst from TD Cowen, maintained the Buy rating on Air Canada. The associated price target was raised to C$26.00.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Thomas Fitzgerald CFA has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that point to improving fundamentals and earnings power for Air Canada. He highlights solid revenue momentum, particularly in corporate and international travel, along with expectations for higher loads and yields in the first half of FY26, supported by strong trans-Atlantic demand and stabilizing Pacific pricing.

He also points to margin expansion potential as unit costs normalize after near-term pressures from labor and fleet investments, with new aircraft expected to deliver scale and efficiency benefits in FY27–FY28. In addition, he notes supportive capital allocation through the NCIB, growth opportunities in corporate and cargo volumes as Canada diversifies trade, and a robust Aeroplan franchise, all of which underpin his increased C$26 price target based on a 2027 EV/EBITDA multiple.

According to TipRanks, Fitzgerald CFA is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 27.3% and a 79.55% success rate. Fitzgerald CFA covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as American Airlines, Alaska Air, and Delta Air Lines.

In another report released on February 13, RBC Capital also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a C$25.00 price target.

data.title: Balanced Near-Term Constraints and Long-Term Optionality Justify Hold Rating and Fair Valuation
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Jason Gabelman has maintained their neutral stance on TTE stock, giving a Hold rating on February 12.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jason Gabelman has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to both near-term guidance and longer-term opportunities. While fourth-quarter results and year-end leverage metrics aligned with expectations and free cash flow slightly outperformed, the company’s 2026 cash flow outlook came in below his prior projections, limiting upside in the near term. He also notes that buybacks are likely to remain constrained by management’s decision not to stretch the balance sheet, even as recent transactions shift the free cash flow trough earlier.

At the same time, Gabelman acknowledges significant strategic optionality beyond 2030 from the expanding power business and promising exploration upside in Namibia, as well as a sizeable free cash flow uplift expected from Suriname later in the decade. These longer-dated growth drivers support a higher price target based on a reduced discount rate and firmer confidence in cash generation through 2030, but their timing and execution risks keep the risk‑reward balanced rather than compelling. As a result, he concludes that the shares are fairly valued, warranting a Hold rating despite attractive long-term projects on the horizon.

In another report released on February 12, J.P. Morgan also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a €58.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: BitGo: Core Institutional Crypto Infrastructure Benefiting From Regulated DeFi and Tokenized Assets Growth
data.description:
BitGo Holdings, Inc. Class A (BTGO) has received a new Buy rating, initiated by Citi analyst, Peter Christiansen.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Peter Christiansen has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to BitGo’s strategic positioning in institutional crypto infrastructure. He views the company as a core hub for on-chain finance, evolving from a pure custody provider into a broader workflow and infrastructure platform that can capture a larger share of client spending as digital assets mature.

Christiansen also argues that BitGo is well placed to benefit from the growth of regulated DeFi and tokenized real‑world assets, supported by its security-focused, multi‑signature custody and strong compliance framework. Despite trimming near‑term revenue and EBITDA estimates to reflect recent crypto volatility, he believes the company’s projected high-teens margins and roughly 30% medium‑term revenue growth justify the $18 target price and Buy/High Risk rating.

Christiansen covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as Strategy, Bullish, and Pagaya Technologies Ltd. According to TipRanks, Christiansen has an average return of -0.2% and a 49.53% success rate on recommended stocks.
